title: rosa canina villosula
intro: Rosa canina villosula, also known as the hairy dog rose, is a shrubby rose plant native to Europe and parts of Asia, characterized by its thorny stems and clusters of fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health due to its high content of vitamins C and E, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa canina villosula include its potential to support eye health, improve digestion, and act as an antioxidant, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Rosa canina villosula often include infusions, tinctures, and teas, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
